title: Events, Observability, Capacity, Inventory, and Cost Control · Execution Trace: Capacity Cost Alerts
summary: Operators have high-cardinality object-key metrics, duplicate unordered events, incomplete request traces, no inventory reconciliation, and no alert tied to a user or cost outcome. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capacity cost alerts increment in storage telemetry, inventory, capacity, and cost model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Forecast request, byte, concurrency, part, KMS, origin, egress, log, and budget limits and connect alerts to admission, degradation, and owner actions.
Use S3 Inventory, Storage Lens, analytics, and sampled reconciliation for object estate, encryption, replication, lifecycle, ownership, and cost evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Capacity Cost Alerts · Theory Capacity Cost Alerts
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2436 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to handle duplicate unordered events with bounded telemetry cardinality and cost; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Forecast request, byte, concurrency, part, KMS, origin, egress, log, and budget limits and connect alerts to admission, degradation, and owner actions.
